concerns.text: Task T021 implements a 'Static Parameter' model, explicitly deviating from Spec FR-005 which mandates a random walk model (θₜ ~ Normal(θₜ₋₁, σₜ²)). There is no task covering the implementation of the Spec's required random walk architecture, leaving FR-005 unimplemented.

Task T026 implements the Diebold-Mariano test, but the Plan explicitly rejects this method in favor of Hierarchical Meta-Analysis. There is no task covering the implementation of the Spec's required Diebold-Mariano test with Westfall-Young correction (FR-006, SC-003) as mandated by the Spec, creating a gap where the Spec's success criterion cannot be met.

Task T009 attempts to fetch RealClearPolitics (RCP) data but allows proceeding with a warning if it fails. Spec FR-001 mandates ingesting data from *both* FiveThirtyEight and RCP. There is no task that enforces the mandatory ingestion of RCP data or handles the scenario where RCP is unavailable as a blocking failure, leaving FR-001 potentially unfulfilled.

Task T027 implements 'Hierarchical Meta-Analysis' as an 'alternative' method. While the Plan suggests this, the Spec (SC-003) strictly defines the success criterion as Diebold-Mariano tests. The task description is ambiguous about whether this replaces the Spec's requirement or is an addition, potentially causing confusion on which method satisfies SC-003.
